title: cuphea pustulata
intro: Cuphea pustulata, also known as the hairy cuphea or hairy wedelia, is a type of flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cuphea pustulata include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Cuphea pustulata can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
